spec_match.rationale: Chromium-catches-up feature — the spec already documents the behaviour, so the value of the demo is the interop story. The concept exercises one corner but the before/after comparison isn't always crisp.

interactive.rationale: 2 buttons / 1 inputs / 1 selects / 0 ranges driving live JS — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on counter, unique).

edge_cases.rationale: Some fallback / error handling is present but corner cases like removed-element behaviour, denied permission flow, or no-support browsers aren't all covered.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5153386492198912 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: difference between events and interactions
openQuestions.detail: The performance-event-timing-interactioncount feature's headline framing — performance.interactionCount — total count of distinct user interactions (gestures), not events — calls out "difference between events and interactions". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: INP budget tracking as a use case
openQuestions.detail: The performance-event-timing-interactioncount feature's headline framing — performance.interactionCount — total count of distinct user interactions (gestures), not events — calls out "INP budget tracking as a use case". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

summary: interaction-counter under performance-event-timing-interactioncount: 4 pass / 2 partial / 0 fail. Strong interactive surface, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
